channel.title: Gatsby Starter Blog RSS Feed
channel.description: A starter blog demonstrating what Gatsby can do.

item.title: My political opinions
item.description: Nevermind, I'm actually just going to write about what I thought about the 2007 Pixar masterpiece Ratatouille. Now, there are many reasons…
item.content:encoded:
Nevermind, I'm actually just going to write about what I thought about the 2007 Pixar masterpiece Ratatouille.

Now, there are many reasons why

item.title: How to make a free blog as a college student
item.description: This is how you can make a free blog as a college student. Or maybe a portfolio or something. Methods Website builder - Services such as…
item.content:encoded:
This is how you can make a free blog as a college student. Or maybe a portfolio or something.

Methods

  1. Website builder - Services such as Wordpress, Wix, Squarespace are all great non-code alternatives.

  2. Github pages + Gatsby - What I'll be going over today. This requires minimal coding experience and is a pretty fun first project to try. I'll try to go over everything in as great as detail as possible. For more advanced users who know there way around a terminal and GitHub, follow these links: Gatsby + Linking Gatsby to Github

item.title: Hello World 2.0
item.description: This is my first post on my new blog! First off, it's way too hard to make a blog. You would think something so integral and simple to the…
item.content:encoded: This is my first post on my new blog! First off, it's way too hard to make a blog. You would think something so integral and simple to the internet would be really quick to spin up. But, there are actually a couple of steps involved. I guess overall, the steps were pretty quick. All I did was download a gatsby template and then host it on GitHub pages. But I don't know, I expected to do it in a much smoother way. In fact! I should write a blog post about how to make a free blog as a student.
